title: Productivity and economic growth
description: An exercise providing students with the opportunity to demonstrate their understanding of economic productivity and growth.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best defines the economic concept of *standard of living*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the amount of money individuals have in their savings accounts
the level of wealth, comfort, material goods, and necessities available to a certain socioeconomic class in a certain geographic area
the total amount of goods and services produced by a country in a given year
the unemployment rate in a specific region

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best defines the economic concept of *productivity*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the total amount of goods and services produced by a country in a given year
the ratio of output produced to the inputs used in production
the number of hours worked by employees in a specific industry
the level of satisfaction of employees in their workplace

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best defines the economic concept of *capital goods*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: goods that are used by consumers to satisfy their needs and wants
goods that are used in the production of other goods and services
goods that are imported from other countries to boost the economy
goods that are sold at a higher price due to their brand value

assessment_items.question_content:
Sarah runs a small bakery and recently invested in a new dough mixer that allows her to make twice as much dough in the same amount of time.

**How is Sarah's investment in the new dough mixer likely to affect the costs of production at her bakery?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The fixed costs of the bakery will decrease significantly because of the new equipment.
The variable costs per unit of output will increase because of the new equipment.
The total costs of production will remain the same, but the cost per unit will decrease due to higher productivity.
The overall costs of production will increase as she produces more dough with the new equipment.

assessment_items.question_content:
A shoe manufacturing company in Virginia recently invested in advanced machinery, which allows their workers to produce twice as many shoes in the same amount of time.

**How is this increase in productivity likely to affect the company's costs of production?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The company's costs of production will increase because they are producing more shoes.
The company's costs of production will decrease because each shoe now takes less time and fewer resources to produce.
The company's costs of production will stay the same because the number of workers remains unchanged.
The company's costs of production will increase because advanced machinery is expensive to maintain.

assessment_items.question_content:
David and Jasmine own a farm in rural Virginia, growing a variety of fruits and vegetables. To cope with increasing demand and competition, they decided to  invest in modern farming equipment that is now allowing them to produce twice as many crops as before without needing additional labor.

**What is the likely impact of David and Jasmine's increase in productivity on their farm's costs of production?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: David and Jasmine will see a decrease in production costs because of the new equipment.
David and Jasmine will see an increase in production costs due to an increase in productivity.
Production costs at David and Jasmine's farm will not change, even with the new equipment.
Total costs of production will permanently increase because of the cost of new equipment.

assessment_items.question_content:
A local manufacturer in Virginia has implemented new technology that significantly increases the productivity of its workers, allowing them to produce more goods in less time.

**How is the increase in productivity at the local manufacturer likely to affect the standard of living for the workers and the local community?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The standard of living will decrease because workers will have to work more hours to produce the same amount of goods.
The standard of living will increase as workers may receive higher wages and the community benefits from lower prices and more job opportunities.
The standard of living will remain the same because productivity improvements only affect the company's profits.
The standard of living will decrease because the company will likely lay off workers due to increased efficiency.

assessment_items.question_content:
Amina and her family live in Alexandria, Virginia where there has been a significant investment in technology and infrastructure, leading to increased productivity in local businesses.  

**How is the increase in productivity likely to affect the standards of living for Amina and her neighbors?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Their standard of living will increase due to better opportunities in the community.
Their standard of living will decrease as goods will be more expensive.
Their standard of living will not change, as it is not related to productivity.
Their standard of living will decrease because unemployment will go up when new technology is utilized.

assessment_items.question_content:
Over the past few years, Carlos and his family have noted significant technological advancements in his neighborhood. Local businesses have adopted new technologies, leading to higher productivity.

**How is the increase in productivity likely to affect the standards of living for Carlos and his neighbors?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The standards of living will increase for Carlos and his neighbors due to the increased productivity.
The standards of living will decrease for Carlos and his neighbors because of the increased productivity.
The increased productivity will not change the standard of living for Carlos and his neighbors.
Carlos and his neighbors will experience more economic inequality because of the increased productivity.

assessment_items.question_content:
A school district in Virginia has decided to invest in new technology for classrooms, provide advanced training for teachers, and upgrade educational facilities. 

**How are these investments in human capital, capital goods, and technology likely to affect overall productivity within the school district?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The productivity of students and teachers will remain the same because these investments do not directly impact learning outcomes.
The productivity of students and teachers will improve as better technology, advanced training, and upgraded facilities create a more effective learning environment.
The investments will only benefit the school's reputation but will not affect the actual productivity of students and teachers.
The productivity of students will decrease as they adjust to the new technology and changes in their learning environment.

assessment_items.question_content:
In Charlottesville, Virginia, a community center run by entrepreneurs is upgrading staff training, purchasing new equipment, and implementing software to streamline operations. 

**How will the investments in human capital, capital goods, and technology affect the overall productivity of the community center?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Productivity will increase because of investments in human capital, capital goods, and technology.
Productivity will decrease, as more time will need to be spent at the center to implement these investments.
Productivity will not change, as these investments are not related to it.
Productivity will decrease, as these investments have high costs.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jamila runs a tech startup in Hampton, Virginia that develops educational apps. She has decided to fund training programs for her employees, upgrade the office furniture, and develop a software platform to streamline project management and collaboration. 

**How will the investments in human capital, capital goods, and technology affect the productivity of Jamila's tech startup?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The investment will lead to an increase in productivity, as more resources will be available to the employees.
Productivity will decrease because employees will have less time to work.
There will be no change in productivity as these changes focus on the office and equipment.
Productivity will decrease as new systems take longer to use.
